Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Vetigastropoda; Trochida; Trochoidea; Trochidae; Cantharidinae; Steromphala
Assembly: The assembly of Steromphala pennanti (xgStePenn6.1) is based on 71X of PacBio data and 162X of Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 67 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 11.66 Mb (with the largest being 7.95 Mb). Additionally, 272 regions totaling 49.16 Mb (with the largest being 6.07 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions and and 4 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 4.29 Mb and 0.31 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 1.47 Mb and 0.11 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly daSolNigr1.hap1.1 is based on 59x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Description: Caenorhabditis brenneri strain CFB2252 genome assembly, chromosome: IV
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Nematoda; Chromadorea; Rhabditida; Rhabditina; Rhabditomorpha; Rhabditoidea; Rhabditidae; Peloderinae; Caenorhabditis
Assembly: CAEBRE_CFB2252 assembly of Caenorhabditis brenneri. The genome was generated form a highly inbred strain, CFB2252, using PacBio CLR reads with FALCON. Contigs were polished with Illumina reads using Pilon, scaffolded using Hi-C data with the 3D-DNA pipeline, and polished again with PacBio HiFi reads, the assembly gaps were closed with PacBio HiFi and polished CLR reads using LR_Gapcloser and TGS-GapCloser. For genome annotation, we utilized RNA-seq and Iso-Seq data along with protein sequences from related Caenorhabditis nematodes.

Assembly: The assembly wsOasSpea1.1 is based on 34x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There are some large regions of heterozygous inversions that couldn't been resolved on chromosome 7 (40.6 - 50.6 Mbp), chromosome 9 (31.6 - 50 mbp) and chromosome 14 (19.6 - 26 and 32.3 - 40.4 Mbp).

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Vetigastropoda; Trochida; Trochoidea; Trochidae; Cantharidinae; Steromphala
Assembly: The assembly xgSteCine2.1 is based on 42x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Large inversions between sister chromatids can be seen on chromosome 5 29.7-60.7mb and chromosome 11 17.7-39.7mb. Possible inversions on chromosome 11 3.4-39.4 and 18-66mb.
